message.content:
Test file: src/mhpco.spec.ts
Failing test: "should return 60 G for three components instead of 75 G"
Expected behavior: When exactly 3 identical components are insured, they get a special base premium of 60 G (instead of 3 × 25 = 75 G). Total premium = 60 G + processing fee
Current error: Expected 60, received 95
Implementation file: src/mhpco.ts

The building block rule applies only when there are exactly 3 identical items. Implement minimal code to detect this and apply the 60 G base premium for the 3-component block.

EXPERIMENT MODE: Run autonomously, proceed to Refactor after completion.

content.text: I'll implement the minimal code to make this test pass. Let me start by examining the current state of the test and implementation files.

Now I understand the current state. The test expects exactly 3 components to return 60 G (no processing fee shown in test), but the current implementation adds a processing fee. Let me check what the actual current error is by running the test:

content.text: Perfect! All tests pass, including the previously failing test. Let me verify the implementation one more time:

Green Phase Complete:

Implementation: Added a building block rule check that detects exactly 3 identical components and returns 60 G base premium (no processing fee added for this special case).

Result: All tests now pass (9 passing, 30 todo)

Approach: This is minimal because:
- Only checks for the exact condition: 3 items AND all are "component" type
- Returns the hardcoded 60 G value demanded by the test
- Does not add processing fees to the 3-component bundle (test expects exactly 60)
- Does not implement any logic for other quantities or item types
- Does not change existing functionality for 1, 2, or 4+ components

The implementation in `/home/experimenter/experiments/runs/2026-05-09_17-39-40_claim-office-example-mapping_v4-exact-subagents_haiku-4-5-no-thinking/src/mhpco.ts` (lines 33-36) adds just 4 lines of code to handle this specific case before falling back to the normal calculation path.

Proceeding to Refactor phase.
